data.title: Hanwha Aerospace Set to Get Earnings Lift From New Contract WinsMarket Talk
data.description: Hanwha Aerospace is poised for an earnings lift from the latest new contract wins in Poland and India, Nomura analyst Eon Hwang writes in a note. Hwang raises his operating-profit estimate for the South Korean defense company by 0.4% for 2025 and by 0.9% for 2026, factoring in two separate contracts secured in April to supply Poland, with Krab self-propelled howitzer chassis products, and India, with K-9 self-propelled howitzer components. He expects Hanwha to secure a total of KRW10.2-trillion new contracts overseas in 2025. Nomura raises its target price for the stock to KRW910,000 from KRW880,000, maintaining a buy rating. Shares are 5.4% higher at KRW728,000. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: Luxshare Precision Industry Eyes Overseas Expansion Amid US Tariffs; Shares Jump 7%
data.description:
Luxshare Precision Industry is exploring options to shift more production outside China, including to the US, in response to newly imposed US tariffs, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Chair Wang Laichun said that while the tariffs are expected to have minimal financial impact, the company may pause some China investments and increase operations abroad, according to the report.

"If there is a commercial guarantee and we are able to conduct a good evaluation, we do not rule out having some products being localized to meet the needs of the US market," Reuters cited Wang as saying.

Shares of the iPhone assembler rose 7% in recent trade.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: J&T's Parcel Volume Rises 31% in Q1; Shares Jump 3%
data.description:
J&T Global Express' total parcel volume increased 31% to about 6.60 billion in the first quarter from 5.03 billion in the year-ago period, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The delivery company's average daily parcel volume jumped 33% to 73.3 million during the three-month period from 55.3 million a year earlier, the filing said.

Parcel volume from China rose 27% to 4.98 billion from 3.94 billion a year earlier, while parcel volume from Southeast Asia increased 50% to 1.54 billion from 1.03 billion in the year-ago period.

Shares in J&T jumped 3% during Thursday morning trading.

data.title: SEA Markets Open Higher as Trump Pauses Reciprocal Tariffs For Most Countries
data.description:
Southeast Asian markets opened higher on Thursday after US President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on higher tariffs for most countries.

While the US paused tariff hikes for most nations, it raised duties on Chinese goods to 125% from 104% after China lifted its tariffs on US imports to 84%.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI, the main gauge of Malaysian stocks, also opened significantly higher on Friday. The benchmark index began the day at 1,464.30, up 63.71 points or 4.55% from the previous day's close.

The Taiwan Stock Exchange Weighted Index (TAIEX) opened at 18,256.47, up from the previous close of 17,391.76.

The Philippine Stock Exchange Index (PSEi) opened 3.19% higher at 6,197.72, from the previous close of 6,006.34.

Meanwhile, the Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) opened at 6,270.61, up 5.07% from the previous close of 5,967.99.

Whereas, Thailand's SET Index gained 4.39% on Friday's open at 1,135.79, from the previous close of 1,088.18.

data.title: Nestlé to Audit Water Filtration Practices as Mineral Water Brand Perrier Raises Questions
data.description:
Nestlé told a French Senate hearing on Wednesday that it is launching an audit of its water filtration process amid questions regarding the authenticity of its Perrier brand, which is marketed as a mineral water, Bloomberg News reported the same day.

Chief Executive Officer Laurent Freixe disclosed during the hearing that French government-commissioned hydrogeologists issued an unfavorable opinion regarding Perrier's use of springs for its "natural mineral waters." Local authorities will consider the opinion regarding the renewal of the company's license to use the springs in Vergèze, France, as the source for its Perrier mineral water.

In a statement to Bloomberg, the Swiss food and drinks giant maintained "that all our Perrier natural mineral waters are safe to drink and their unique minerality has always been preserved as indicated on the label."

data.title: Advance Residence Investment Secures 8.4 Billion Yen in Financings to Repay Debts and Redeem Bonds
data.description:
Advance Residence Investment has secured four separate debt financings totaling 8.4 billion yen, including multiple green loans, with drawdowns scheduled between April 14 to April 30.

A 1.5 billion yen refinancing will replace maturing loans from Nippon Life and Yamaguchi Bank while another 2 billion yen green loan facility (April 18) from Mizuho Bank tied to a DBJ 5-star certified student housing property.

Further, a 2 billion yen bond refinancing (April 21) through regional banks to redeem 10-year corporate bonds while a 2.9 billion yen mixed facility (April 30) featuring green loans from Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank for a CASBEE A-ranked office tower.

All new financings maintain Reit's unsecured, non-guaranteed structure while extending debt maturities by 5-10 years, with interest rates ranging from base plus 0.155% to base plus 0.26%.

The proceeds will primarily refinance existing debt obligations maturing in 2025, including 2 billion yen in 10-year bonds issued in 2015 at 0.726% and various bank loans with rates between 0.18-0.996%.

data.title: British Business - April 10
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the business pages of British newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

The Times

- British medical property owner REIT Assura has agreed to a 1.61 billion-pound ($2.06 billion) takeover bid from KKR and Stonepeak Partners after rejecting a lower cash-and-stock proposal from peer Primary Health Properties .

- British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will formally approve investment for the construction of Sizewell C in Suffolk before the spending review in June.

The Guardian

- Britain and India have agreed 90% of their free trade agreement, businesses were told this week.

- U.S. President Donald Trump's unfolding trade war makes it even more "imperative" for Britain to improve its post-Brexit trading relations with the European Union (EU), British chancellor Rachel Reeves has said.

The Telegraph

- British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has inked a 50 billion-pound ($64.21 billion) deal with Comcast for a new Hollywood theme park in Bedford, which will be built on 480 acres of land and open to visitors 365 days a year.

- Rupert Murdoch's REA Group has abandoned a fresh bid for Rightmove after U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs plunged global markets into chaos. The property group had been considering a renewed swoop six months after its last 6.2 billion-pound offer was rebuffed.

Sky News

- Rupert Murdoch's News UK is close to agreeing a deal to combine its influencer marketing division, The Fifth, with Brave Bison .

- Proxy advisor ISS has recommended that shareholders vote against Unilever's remuneration report at its annual meeting later this month over its new chief executive's multi-million pound pay package.

($1 = 0.7788 pounds)

data.title: Hang Seng Index Last 1.9% Higher at 20641.05
China's Top Leaders to Meet Thursday to Discuss Stimulus, Bloomberg Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• China's top leaders are slated to meet Thursday to discuss extra stimulus measures in the wake of steep U.S. tariffs, Bloomberg reported, citing unnamed sources.
  • The ad-hoc meeting will focus on support measures for housing, consumer spending and technological innovation, the people told Bloomberg.
  • Financial regulators and other government bodies will also meet to discuss steps to stabilize the markets, according to Bloomberg.
  • The State Council Information Office didn't respond to Bloomberg's request for a comment.

Full story: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-04-10/top-china-leaders-to-meet-on-stimulus-after-trump-s-tariff-shock

Write to Singapore Editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: *Hong Kong Stocks Trim Early Gains
data.description:
- -

*Hang Seng Tech Index Last 2.5% Higher at 4804.54

data.title: Eni Unveils Governance-focused Alliance with McKinsey & Co., SAP Italy
data.description:
Eni (ENI.MI) launched G∙row, a governance-focused business alliance with management consulting firm McKinsey & Co. and the Italian arm of German software giant SAP .

The Italian energy company said Wednesday that the alliance's objective is to foster the evolution of control and risk management systems in the corporate value chain in a complex global environment.

Eni functions as the lead in the initiative, with McKinsey and SAP Italy as strategic supports. It said the alliance will develop a common framework that promotes scalable control models, self-assessments and comparisons, and organic and adaptive improvements.

The framework will be made available via a complimentary platform. Eni noted that the alliance is also engaging key national and international stakeholders.

data.title: Fertiglobe Shareholders OKs H2 2024 Dividend, Share Buyback
data.description:
Fertiglobe (ADX:FERTIGLB) said Wednesday its shareholders approved a dividend payment of $125 million or 0.055 Emirati dirham per share for the second half of fiscal 2024.

The second-half dividend will be paid within 30 days of the Emirati fertilizer manufacturer and distributor's general assembly to shareholders on record on April 21, 2025. It paid $200 million or 0.09 dirham per share a year ago, taking the company's total dividend for 2024 to $275 million from $475 earlier.

Fertiglobe also said that shareholders and the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange approved its request to repurchase up to 2.5% of its issued shares through a buyback program. The plan also obtained a no-objection confirmation from Abu Dhabi Global Market.

data.title: Singapore Court Discharges Alpha DX from Judicial Management; Winding-Up Order Issued
data.description:
The High Court of Singapore discharged judicial management of Alpha DX and made a winding up order of the company, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Earlier, judicial managers had applied to discharge the judicial management order and place the company into liquidation after failing to secure a rescue plan.

Alpha DX was placed under judicial management as part of a restructuring effort, but the failure to secure a turnaround plan has prompted the shift toward liquidation.

data.title: US assets left behind in relief rally after Trump pauses tariffs
data.description:
Key points:
  • Stocks rally sharply, but US futures turn lower
  • Chinese equities rise even as yuan hits multi-year trough
  • Bond market rout shows signs of stabilising

Asian shares climbed and a manic bond selloff stabilised on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump said he would temporarily lower the hefty duties he had just imposed on dozens of countries.

Following a days-long market rout that erased trillions of dollars from global stocks and jolted U.S. Treasury bonds and the dollar, Trump on Wednesday announced a 90-day pause on many of his new tariffs in a shock reversal.

But U.S. stock futures and the dollar were left out of Thursday's relief rally despite an overnight surge on Wall Street, as investors' confidence in the U.S. administration crumbles and the "sell America" trade heats up.

"The world, political and financial is looking on with horror, not bemusement, at an administration that prioritises the signing of an executive order for more water-power in shower heads, on the same day that the bond market breaks and investors question the long-term credibility of the administration having flip flopped on the largest of their policies, tariffs," said Martin Whetton, head of financial markets strategy at Westpac.

Nasdaq futures slid more than 1% after a brief rally early in the Asian session, while S&P 500 futures sank 0.75%.

Both indexes had clocked their biggest daily percentage gains in more than a decade during Wednesday's cash session.

The dollar fell 0.7% against the yen and 0.6% against the Swiss franc , failing to sustain its sharp overnight jump against the two safe haven currencies, highlighting market uncertainty over the longer term outlook.

"I think the initial move was just massive short cover, and this has given the world a bit of a breathing space, except for China... because markets were starting to price in the worst-case scenario," said Khoon Goh, head of Asia research at ANZ.

"But now that the dust has settled, I think markets will seem to sort of figure out where to go from here."

In the broader market, Japan's Nikkei and European futures were among the standout winners of the rally in Asia.

The Nikkei surged 8%, EUROSTOXX 50 futures and DAX futures climbed roughly 8% each, while FTSE futures jumped 5.4%.

Trump's reversal on the country-specific tariffs is not absolute. A 10% blanket duty on almost all U.S. imports will remain in effect, the White House said. The announcement also does not appear to affect duties on autos, steel and aluminium that are already in place.

He also heaped pressure on China, saying he would raise the tariff on Chinese imports to 125% from the 104% level that came into effect on Wednesday.

China on Wednesday raised additional duties on American products to 84% and imposed restrictions on 18 U.S. companies, mostly in defence-related industries.

Yet investors for now seemed to view the latest escalation of Sino-U.S. trade tensions with a narrow lens, choosing merely to focus on the 90-day window Trump has granted to the rest of the world.

China's CSI300 blue-chip index was up 1%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index advanced 2.2%.

"I guess at least the relief is now global trade won't grind to a complete halt," said Wong Kok Hoong, head of equity sales trading at Maybank.

"The China + 1 supply chain route (is) still intact. As the rest of the world will be at workable 10% tariffs for 90 days, companies/businesses have time/alternatives to adjust supply chain routes."

But the move in the yuan painted a different story as the onshore unit fell to its weakest level since December 2007 at 7.3518 per dollar.

Prior to market opening, the People's Bank of China (PBOC) set the midpoint rate , around which the yuan is allowed to trade in a 2% band, at its lowest level since September 11, 2023.

BONDS SELLOFF

A steep selloff in bonds this week also showed some signs of easing on Thursday.

The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield dropped to 4.2774%, having touched a high of 4.5150% in the previous session and rising some 13 basis points.

A violent U.S. Treasury selloff in the previous sessions, evoking the COVID-era "dash for cash", had reignited fears of fragility in the world's biggest bond market.

"Sticky inflation, a patient (Federal Reserve), potential foreign buyer boycotts, hedge fund deleveraging, rebalancing out of bonds into cash, and an illiquid Treasury market are all reasons why Treasury yields continue to move higher," said Lawrence Gillum, chief fixed income strategist at LPL Financial.

Fed policymakers signalled they will not be quick to ride to the rescue with interest rate cuts because they expect higher tariffs to boost inflation, even as they worry Trump's trade policy could deal a blow to economic growth, minutes of the central bank's mid-March meeting out on Wednesday showed.

Markets are now pricing in just about 80 basis points of rate cuts by December, down from more than 100 bps earlier in the week. (0#USDIRPR)

Elsewhere, oil prices fell as investors fretted about the worsening U.S.-China trade war.

Spot gold extended its climb and was last up 1.5% at $3,128.92 an ounce.

data.title: Market Chatter: UOB Trims Singapore's Growth Forecast to 1.5%
data.description:
United Overseas Bank, UOB, has revised Singapore's GDP forecast for 2025 to 1.5% after previously forecasting 2.5%, Singapore Business Review reported Wednesday.

Similarly, the Ministry of Trade and Industry is likely to follow suit and could revise the current forecast range of between 1.0% and 3.0%, as the city-state weighs the impact of US tariffs, the report added.

The government is also putting plans in place if domestic conditions go south, with responses such as cash or voucher distribution, employment support on the cards, the report noted.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Iron Ore Prices Rise After Trump's Tariff PauseMarket Talk
data.description: Iron ore prices are higher in early Asia trade, following a rebound in sentiment after Trump paused the hikes on his tariffs on most countries. Prices are still largely being moved by the macro uncertainties, GF Futures analysts write in a note. In the short term, the prices will remain volatile amid geopolitical tensions and trade disputes, they add. If the exports are dragged by the higher U.S. tariffs, high iron ore inventories may pressure prices lower, they say. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is up 2.6% at CNY703.50 a ton.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Bremworth CEO Steps Down; Interim Successor Named
data.description:
Carpet manufacturer Bremworth disclosed the departure of Chief Executive Greg Smith, effective April 11, according to a Thursday filing with the New Zealand bourse.

The board appointed Craig Woolford as interim CEO on a 12-month contract while the search for a permanent successor is underway, the filing stated.

Smith, who has been in the role for four years, will remain with the company until April 30 to assist with the transition, per the filing.

Bremworth's shares were up nearly 2% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Netwealth Bull Wary on Margin ExpectationsMarket Talk
data.description: Netwealth's bull at Citi warns that analysts might be setting their fiscal 2026 earnings-margin expectations a bit too high. Analyst Siraj Ahmed cautions that the one negative in the Australian wealth platform's 3Q update was the potential for higher annual costs. He writes in a note that the costs are discretionary and can be managed down if necessary, but he nonetheless sees some risk to analysts' margin expectations. He raises his cost-growth forecast and expects Ebitda margin to be flat at about 50%, compared with an average analyst forecast of 52%. He maintains his buy rating on the stock after Netwealth beat his net-custodial-flow forecast by 3%. Citi lifts its target price by 3.0% to A$27.30. Shares are up 13% at A$25.47. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0349 GMT - Netwealth's bull at Citi warns that analysts might be setting their fiscal 2026 earnings-margin expectations a bit too high. Analyst Siraj Ahmed cautions that the one negative in the Australian wealth platform's 3Q update was the potential for higher annual costs. He writes in a note that the costs are discretionary and can be managed down if necessary, but he nonetheless sees some risk to analysts' margin expectations. He raises his cost-growth forecast and expects Ebitda margin to be flat at about 50%, compared with an average analyst forecast of 52%. He maintains his buy rating on the stock after Netwealth beat his net-custodial-flow forecast by 3%. Citi lifts its target price by 3.0% to A$27.30. Shares are up 13% at A$25.47. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0250 GMT - Samsung SDI's earnings could be hit by weaker-than-expected shipments of electric-vehicle batteries in 2025, Nomura analyst Cindy Park writes in a note. The South Korean battery maker's overall shipments and average selling prices for its products are projected to decline 17% and 4%, respectively, this year due to order cancellations by some customers and lower metal prices, Park says. She cuts her full-year net profit forecast by 52% and expects the company's EPS to fall 54%. Nomura lowers its target price for the stock by 21% to KRW190,000 and keeps a neutral rating. Shares are 4.5% higher at KRW175,600. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0220 GMT - Hong Kong shares are sharply higher in early Asian trading, tracking strong gains on Wall Street overnight. U.S. stock markets recorded their third-biggest advance in post-World War II history after President Trump paused certain tariffs, UOB analysts say in a commentary. Among major stocks, tech companies are leading the gains. Alibaba rises 5.3%, Meituan gains 2.9% and Baidu adds 5.3%. Auto stocks are also higher, with NIO jumping 6.1% and Li Auto climbing 6.8%. The Hang Seng Index rises 4.2% to 21119.67 and the Hang Seng Tech Index is 5.7% higher. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0214 GMT - Indonesia's Jakarta Composite Index is 4.7% higher at 6251.49 in early trade, tracking Wall Street's rally overnight after Trump hit pause on reciprocal tariffs for 90 days, excluding China. The delay could ease immediate pressure on emerging Asia's growth outlook, Barclays analysts say in a note. Still, the 10% universal tariff remains in place for all major economies outside China, posing a continued drag on global trade, they say. Risks linger, especially with new sector-specific tariffs, such as on pharmaceuticals, likely to go ahead, they add. Among gainers, GoTo Gojek adds 13% and Bank Central Asia is 6.3% higher. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0212 GMT - Investors appear to be leaning toward the view that President Trump's so-called reciprocal tariffs will be significantly walked back before a 90-day pause ends, says David Cassidy, head of investment strategy at Wilsons Advisory. "We see this as a significant positive for both the global and U.S. economy and key asset markets relative to the situation 24 hours ago," Cassidy says. Still, significant uncertainty remains, especially when it comes to the outlook for U.S.-China relations, he says. "Despite the rally and improved outlook, some additional required risk premium for U.S. assets is likely permanent or could take years to unwind," adds Cassidy. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0151 GMT - Chinese shares open higher even as U.S.-China trade tensions continue to escalate. After China retaliated with an 84% tariff on U.S. imports, President Trump slapped back with raising tariffs on Chinese goods to 125%. Yet, investors have started to look past the initial tariff shock and pick potential winners. Tech hardware makers that play key roles in Apple's supply chain lead the gains. Foxconn Industrial Internet is 9.6% higher and Luxshare Precision Industry surges 8.9%. Auto stocks rise broadly as well after China's March auto sales rose sharply thanks to ongoing government subsidies and moderating price competition. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is 0.85% higher at 3213.73, the Shenzhen Composite Index rises 2.6% and the ChiNext Price Index is up 3.0%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0142 GMT - Unpredictable U.S. trade policy could weigh on global growth and sentiment, and investors in Malaysia should stay cautious, RHB analyst Alexander Chia says in a note. While Malaysia remains relatively resilient among emerging markets, investor sentiment is likely to stay fragile, he says. Chia advises investors to build a defensive portfolio with higher cash holdings in near term, while selectively accumulating quality domestic-focused stocks on weakness. He sees Trump's tariffs as transactional, allowing room for negotiation but keeping markets on edge. RHB lowers its end-2025 KLCI target to 1650 from 1750. The KLCI is 4.3% higher at 1460.20. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0135 GMT - Posco Future M's heavy exposure to the U.S. electric-vehicle market offers both risks and opportunities, Daiwa Capital analysts Henny Jung and Yoonki Bae write in a note. The South Korean EV battery-material supplier's cathode shipment volume could contract 40% in 2025, they say, given its amplified vulnerability to slower U.S. shipments of battery electric vehicles under potential policy changes by the new Trump administration. On the other hand, its anode-material business could expand faster than expected, thanks to clients' possible import-diversification efforts amid rising uncertainties over U.S. tariffs on China, which accounted for 40% of the 2024 U.S. anode-material imports, they add. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0134 GMT - JB Hi-Fi shares' recent retreat from a record secures the Australian entertainment retailer a new bull at Bell Potter. Analyst Chami Ratnapala raises her recommendation to buy from hold, with the stock having retreated 16% between early February and the end of Monday's session. She makes no changes to her forecasts, but tells clients in a note that she expects JB Hi-Fi's 3Q trading update will show sales growth close to the level seen early in the period. She acknowledges headwinds from a weak Australian dollar, economic uncertainty and promotional activity, but sees further central-bank rate cuts supporting consumer demand from next month. Bell Potter keeps a A$99.00 target price on the stock, which is up 5.5% at A$95.32. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0132 GMT - Brazil's used-vehicle sales seem to be holding up despite recent interest-rate hikes, which Citi analyst Siraj Ahmed points out is good news for Australia-listed classifieds group CAR. Ahmed tells clients in a note that annual growth in used-vehicle sales accelerated to 17% in March, from 12% in February. He says this is positive for CAR's local Webmotors platform, which generates revenue from sales leads. Investors have been worried that higher interest rates in the country could dampen demand, he adds. Citi keeps a buy rating and A$42.00 target price on CAR shares, which are up 5.7% at A$33.05. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0131 GMT - Taiwan stocks surge after President Trump announced a 90-day pause on higher U.S. tariffs for most countries, ending a three-session heavy selloff. The tariff pause offers relief for investors as the island's export-dependent economy is highly exposed to the U.S. TSMC, the world's largest contract chip maker, which counts Nvidia and Apple as major clients, and Foxconn Technology, a major Apple supplier, are each up nearly 10%, the daily limit permitted in the Taiwan market. Quanta Computer and MediaTek gain 9.8% and 9.7%, respectively. Taiwan's benchmark Taiex index is 9.3% higher at 19012.03. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0120 GMT - Catapult Sports International's bull at Bell Potter sees the positives outweighing the negatives despite uncertainty over the potential impact of tariffs on the sports-tech provider. Analyst Chris Savage tells clients in a note that he anticipates a strong annual result, with annual contract value rising 19% on-year to US$103 million. He is also positive that Catapult can continue to deliver on cross-selling, especially with new product launches on the way. Savage points out that tariffs will only impact Catapult's hardware business, which is relatively small. Other than the Australian dollar, all currencies in which Catapult does business have held up well against the U.S. dollar, he adds. Bell Potter keeps a buy rating and A$4.00 target price on the stock, which is up 11% at A$3.35. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Australian shares rise as global markets rebound on eased U.S. tariffs
data.description:
** Australia's S&P/ASX 200 soars as much as 6.3% to 7842.9 points, its biggest intraday gain since the onset of the COVID pandemic

** Country's benchmark tracking rally seen in markets around the world after U.S. President Donald Trump eases some of his tariffs, healing soured investor sentiment

** Index heavyweights Miners and Financials lead the boost, up 6.4% and 4.5% respectively

** Top gainers include Zip co (up 20.9%) , miner Sandfire Resources (up 16.8%)

** YTD, benchmark down 5.4% YTD

data.title: New York Times business news - April 10
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the New York Times business pages. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- The House of Representatives on Wednesday approved resolutions striking down a $5 cap on most bank overdraft fees and overturning a rule that would have given a federal regulator greater oversight powers over payment apps run by large technology companies.

- Beijing on Wednesday aimed the latest blow in the escalating trade war between the United States and China, by raising new tariffs on all American imports by 84%.

- Upstart razor company Harry's plans to announce that it is rebranding as Mammoth Brands, a personal grooming conglomerate, as it sets its sights on deal-making — and, potentially, an initial public offering.

- NASA will prioritize sending American astronauts to Mars, President Trump's nominee, Jared Isaacman to lead the space agency told a Senate committee on Wednesday.

data.title: Neste Signs New EUR1.3 Billion Revolver
data.description:
Neste signed a new multi-currency revolving credit facility totaling 1.3 billion euros with a syndicate of 13 banks.

The facility replaces an existing 1.2 billion-euro revolver from December 2019, according to a Wednesday release. It has a tenor of five years with two one-year extension options.

The sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel company plans to use the facility for general corporate purposes.

data.title: RITES Signs MOU with DP World Collaborate on Logistics Infrastructure Development
data.description:
RITES has entered into a memorandum of understanding with Dubai-based DP World to explore opportunities for collaboration in the logistics infrastructure development space, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The two companies will jointly scout for opportunities in infrastructure development, including ports, multimodal logistics parks, free trade zones, rail connectivity projects, and logistics infrastructure services.

data.title: Australia's Immuron gains on highest annual sales for its Travelan drug
data.description:
** Shares of Immuron rise 11.7% to A$0.067

** Stock set for its strongest session since January 14, if current trend holds

** The biopharmaceutical co says it has exceeded full-year sales of A$5 million ($3.08 million) for its Travelan drug, marking the co's highest annual sales

** About 437,320 shares change hands, 4.2x the 30-day average of 103,595 shares

** Stock down 15.2% YTD, including current session's moves

($1 = 1.6215 Australian dollars)

data.title: HD Hyundai, Huntington Ingalls Sign Deal to Collaborate on Defense Ship Production; Shares Rise 4%
data.description:
HD Hyundai and Huntington Ingalls Industries signed a memorandum of understanding to collaborate in ship production for defense and commercial projects, Huntington Ingalls said earlier this week.

The partnership aims to enhance technological innovation and production efficiency by combining the expertise of both shipbuilders.

Shares of HD Hyundai jumped more than 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Finance House Unit Secures Regulatory Nod for Provision of Deposits, Credit Services
data.description:
Finance House's (ADX:FH) wholly owned unit secured approval from the Financial Services Regulatory Authority to accept deposits and provide credit services in the United Arab Emirates.

The subsidiary, located within Abu Dhabi financial hub ADGM, will begin operations in May, the financial services company said Wednesday.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Information Technology Sector Leads Rebound as All Sectors Trade in Green
data.description:
Information technology stocks lead a broad-based rebound on the Australian Securities Exchange, surging past 7% at midday Thursday. All ASX sectors were trading in the green.

Stocks on Wall Street soared in a relief rally after US President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on tariffs above the 10% base duty imposed on imports into the US for all countries except for China. The technology stocks-heavy Nasdaq Composite surged 12%, while the S&P 500 climbed 9.5%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average soared 7.9%, their biggest single-day gains in years.

Eroad received notice that UBS Group and its affiliates are no longer substantial holders of the company, effective Wednesday. UBS Group became ERD's substantial holder on March 19.

The energy sector rose about 7%, and the materials sector climbed 6%.

data.title: Australia's Helloworld Travel rises on plans to buy NZ-based co
data.description:
** Helloworld Travel rises 5.4% to A$1.465; set for steepest intraday pct gain since August 2023

** Co announces it will acquire New Zealand-based private co Barlow Travel Group

** Deal expected to be completed by this month

** No financial details have been disclosed

** Stock down 25.1% YTD, including moves in current session
